name: Turkey Spaghetti Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat olive oil in a saucepan over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and garlic, stirring until lightly browned.
2. Stir in the dried basil and thyme. Cook for 1 minute.
3. Add the chopped cooked turkey and canned tomatoes. Stir to combine.
4. Pour in the tomato paste, reserving a small amount of pasta cooking water if desired for consistency.
5. Bring to a simmer, stirring occasionally. Cook for 20 to 25 minutes until flavors meld and sauce thickens slightly.
6. While the sauce simmers, cook the noodles or macaroni according to package instructions.
7. Once the noodles are done, drain and add them to the sauce. Stir to combine.
1/6 recipe not including noodles - 215 calories, 3 lean meat, 2 vegetable exchanges
9 grams carbohydrate, 22 grams protein, 10 grams fat, 190 mg sodium, 627 mg potassium, 54 mg cholesterol.
Source: Am. Diabetes Assoc. Holiday Cookbook by Betty Wedman, 1986
Shared but not tested by Elizabeth Rodier, Nov 93
